text:
Market today: Mild risk-on tone. China tech export demand beating expectations is the real driver for your book - chip names get a direct tailwind when Chinese factories are buying. Inflation staying hot in Europe keeps rate-cut hopes muted globally, which is a soft headwind for growth/tech multiples. Nothing screaming danger, but nothing screaming chase either.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one to watch - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF that decays daily, and a 7% loss in a leveraged name on margin is real pain if it slides further; stop at $200 is wide, consider tightening. SPCX (+0.53%) is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - margin amplifies any sudden drop, manage size manually. SMH (-0.69%) is minor but confirms chips are still finding their footing.

Watch: 1) SOXL - does it hold above $200 stop, or does weakness in SMH drag it down? 2) Any follow-through from China factory data into chip names mid-session.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is SOXL's leveraged decay eating you alive - that's the one position demanding active attention.
plays: SOXL: Consider tightening your stop from $200 toward $215-220 given the -7% loss on a 3x leveraged name on margin - decay and drawdowns compound fast here.
SPCX: Keep size very small; no add until price history builds. If it dips hard, margin exposure makes this one easy to cut first.
No new buys today - China data is a tailwind but chip setups like AMAT/KLAC are in daily premium (chasing territory); wait for a pullback to scanner entries before adding.
